title: SenML codec allows multiple value fields violating RFC 8428
description:
File: python/src/lichen/senml/codec.py
Lines: 117-128 (pack), 131-154 (unpack)

RFC 8428 Section 4.5 states: 'There MUST be at most one of each field in a SenML Record, except for v* (all value fields) there MUST be at most one.'

The codec allows creating and accepting records with multiple value fields (v, vs, vb, vd) present simultaneously:
- pack() serializes all value fields if set
- unpack() accepts records with multiple value fields without error

Example violation:
  SenmlRecord(n='x', v=1.0, vs='hello', vb=True)  # packs successfully but violates spec

This causes:
1. Non-compliant SenML packs sent to other implementations may be rejected
2. Receiving such packs from malicious sources leads to undefined behavior (which value wins?)

Fix: Add validation in to_cbor_map() to raise if >1 value field is set, and in from_cbor_map() to reject records with multiple values.
close_reason: Added _VALUE_FIELDS validation in to_cbor_map/from_cbor_map; fixed cbor2 compatibility in unpack. Ruff/mypy/pytest all pass. 3 codereviews complete (no findings).
